title: arranged/arranged-start/arranged-end sugar
manager: Syntactic sugar — `arranged`, `arranged-start`, `arranged-end` — for laying out a linear sequence of states without writing every transition by hand, so a straightforward pipeline reads as a list instead of a wall of arrows. Pure convenience over the core graph, but the kind that makes the extremely common linear-flow case pleasant to write and, more importantly, pleasant to read back six months later.
cs: arranged / arranged-start / arranged-end sugar expands to explicit sequential transitions; §28 decided; fsl#618.
